refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry logs and compare against the customer-defined availability threshold.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup links to maintain availability.
FR4: Provide real-time link status updates to operators and system managers.

candidate_dps: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover
Robust network link monitoring and diagnostics
Adaptive bandwidth management and error correction
Centralized link status aggregation and alerting
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover

potential_sources: Dependency on network infrastructure managed by Safety Services
Interaction with Teleoperation module for link control
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability management but requires well-defined interfaces with Teleoperation and Safety Services for failover coordination.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of multiple communication channels.
Risk of failover delay impacting link availability.
Risk of integration complexity with existing modules.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding explicit monitoring and alerting DPs to complement failover mechanisms.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation link control, failover mechanisms, and link status reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections used for teleoperation communication and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send link status and diagnostics data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and tasking information from NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion due to cross-cutting concerns; limited containment of link availability logic.
Vehicle module depends on link availability but does not contain control logic.
Strong containment of link availability DP; low diffusion risk.
Supports safety alerts related to link failures but does not control link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
Risk of overloading Teleoperation module with multiple responsibilities.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting monitoring and alerting functions into Safety Services if complexity grows.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR and selected DP align well; the DP addresses the key functional need of maintaining link availability with redundancy and failover.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if link monitoring and alerting are not well modularized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Assigning DP to Teleoperation module provides strong containment and clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and failover event logs is feasible within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider explicit sub-FRs and DPs for monitoring and alerting to improve modularity.
Review interface definitions for SafetyAlert to ensure timely safety notifications.

decision.justification: While the decomposition is sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and DPs related to monitoring and alerting is needed to reduce diffusion and clarify responsibilities.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup links to maintain availability.
FR4: Provide real-time link status updates to operators and system managers.

revise.why: To explicitly capture monitoring and alerting functions as separate sub-FRs and DPs, improving modularity and verification.

assumptions: Multiple communication channels are available for redundancy.
Teleoperation module can handle link control and failover logic.
open_risks: Integration complexity with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
Potential delay in failover impacting availability.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating link availability performance.
Failover event logs and timing analysis.
Interface validation between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
